item.title: I put 100's of Hours Into Making this Tiny Tina Cosplay
item.description:
I put 100's of hours into making this Tiny Tina cosplay from the new game Tiny Tina's Wonderlands! I started it months ago and have been working on every tiny detail on the costume. Recently I got to wear it all and do a photoshoot of it. Wig and makeup alone takes over an hour to put on since its so makeup heavy with all the lines and all the pieces on the costume itself.
Some photos from the photoshoot. It was freezing when we did this photoshoot - but in the end I think it was worth it.
I even made an axe.
Photo of the skirt I sewed together, destroyed then painted! My favorite part was painting the unicorn piece. I used super tiny paint pens to get all the fine details.
A few weeks ago I shared with you all my pink Borderlands styled shoe. Well I did recently finish the other shoe (Tina is a lunatic so she wears two completely different shoes). Here is what the other shoe looks like!
And a video of the two shoes on. Can you believe that these shoes were the same style at one point? I had to COMPLETELY take them apart and add a whole bunch of pieces to them to make them look the way they do in the game. I even made custom soles.
Timelapse of painting parts on the shoe. This is 2 hours of painting. I mostly use acrylic paint/paint pens.
A few build highlights of my cosplay.
Another 2 hours of painting for the shoe parts. I put so much into this Tiny Tina cosplay - honestly even being at this stage of the build - I want to make more for it. The build challenged me in ways that no other cosplay had and I thoroughly enjoyed just the process of creating. Tiny Tina's Wonderlands comes out later this month and I know I will have more costume pieces and props I want to make from it. If you are curious... my full build log can be found here: https://twitter.com/MangolooCosplay/status/1432778173939339266 And more of what I am working on at https://www.instagram.com/mangoloo.cos/
item.dc:creator: Mangoloo

item.title: Yup.
item.dc:creator: rpplethroughtheworld

item.title: 46 stupid things. 4 wholesome. Enjoy.
item.description:
Half way. Take a break. Take a breathe. Move on when you're ready.
If you're trying to improve, help others, and be better than yesterday; well you're doing great.
I really like this perspective.
To come back to reality, and before you continue seeing whats wrong with world, I would like to borrow from one of my favorite authors.
The good things are worth fighting for my friends.
item.dc:creator: odinsbeardhammer

item.title: Los Angeles has no problem covering for the rich. The poor are expandable!
item.description: Edit: *expendable, I am aware! I did not pay much attention to my spelling or grammar because I was worried about the post getting suppressed. The post got suppressed once by r/LosAngeles. They called it a duplicate post. The post got brought back up, the same one. There was never a duplicate, especially when they approve which posts make it onto r/LosAngeles. r/iamtotalpieceofshit removed my post after they called it doxxing. I didn't know a rich entrepreneur whose name is plastered everywhere along with their family would be called doxxing. Fuckers banned me! This post is not meant to be judge, jury, and executioner. We live in a society where the rich eat the poor. I am aware! This post is meant to shed light on what's being done behind our backs. I want to know when this happens and not for it to be swept under the rug. https://losangeles.cbslocal.com/2021/02/18/candlelight-vigil-monique-munoz-killed-in-west-la-crash/ Edit 2: Remember folks! We let law and order handle this case even though it always fails us, the poor. We still must follow the law. We need better legislators and regulators! (originally posted by @Stati5tiker on 2021-03-07 20:17:10)
item.dc:creator: ThisTimeLastYear

item.title: A big THANK YOU to the Imgurians who made it possible for all of these Grogus to be donated to the Ronald McDonald House in Cincinnati.
item.description:
The smaller stack was donated by a family I took photos with, last Sunday morning at the fundraiser. They heard the radio interview I did (I think) on Saturday and came to the show on Sunday to take pix with me and the other SW cosplayers and to donate the Grogus specifically for the RMH Cincinnati. Cool, right? All I had to do was pose with them and look unbelievably handsome (and overly modest about it) and for that, 5 kids I'll never meet, get a new SW toy, post-chemo, post-surgery, etc. Totally worth it! The rest of these 70 were purchased from Costco when they went on sale, from $20 down to $15. That means essentially it was a buy 3 get one free dealio and it stretched everyone donations even further. I checked with Tim, the Manager at my local Costco to see if it was okay to purchase 4 cartloads of these at one time. He told me Costco had 9,000 in inventory and it wouldn't be a problem at all. (The next day the pallet was full again.) These will all be donated to the Ronald McDonald House over the next few visits over 2022, along with lightsabers and other SW toys I have yet to purchase with the donations from you cool Dudes and my friends and my family. Thank you again for being so generous. Have a great week and May The Force Be With You!
Just some other pix from last weekend. I have a link if anyone else wants a shirt like the one I'm wearing. I think the RBatSWNN sells them. I don't sell anything. I'm just here to help charities and that is all. Also, I know the movie is not for everyone, so please don't send any hate my way. I like all of the movies and series.
Tim O'Sullivan built Bistan in the center and is cosplaying him. The SW character appeared in Rogue One as a door gunner. Jon Hammer is in the Boba Fett costume he made. Lastly, and this is key, if you saw any of the news last week it is extremely important to know that is me there, not Mark Hamill. I'm referring to this... https://www.newsweek.com/mark-hamill-star-wars-impersonator-viral-video-fluke-skywalker-1684145
Tim is now cosplaying in the C-3PO costume he made.
With some local Rebel Legion volunteers (they were super nice and fun to work with both days) in front of a standard backdrop at the show.
This is me with my happy face. Actually, this is the stare I could give a student in one of my classes if I saw them trying to cheat on a test or bullying/ teasing someone smaller or introverted. I was never mean to a student but I let them know they were doing something they shouldn't be doing, with this silent stare. It worked.
item.dc:creator: imflukeskywalker

item.title: Repost for awarness: Ukraine and trumps impeachment
item.description:
Great comic explaining why trump got impeached the first time for basically blackmailing Ukrainian president Zelenskyy. It's being brought up again with everything going on so I thought I'd spell it out for anyone who missed it the first time.
Sauce: https://www.insider.com/trump-impeachment-graphic-feature-comic-2020-2#-12

item.title: I had enough abuse from 1 person and never again
item.description: I don't advocate for violence and I still don't till this day. But I figure I would share this story in how I stood up to a bully who just didn't know when to quit. This happened back in High School, for 4 years this kid never left me alone. The name calling and teasing just never stopped. From the moment I got onto the buss until I got home was a constant nightmare. It wasn't until my senior year of High School did things get extremely bad. During gym glass one day we were playing flag football when he wouldn't stop shoving me into the ground. I told him to stop because this wasn't a contact sport but he refused. Even the Gym teacher told us no contact. Eventually he pushed me into the metal bleachers outside and my head hit the bleachers. I passed out and woke up in the nurses office with an ice pack on my head. I was lucky I didn't have a concussion. Half way through the school year I took an art class in pottery/sculpting. I was never very artistic but I started to enjoy myself in my crafts that I would construct. One day my bully destroyed one of my art projects that I had been working on for 2 weeks. It was smashed to pieces. There was no way for me to put it back together again. The teacher saw me crying and kicked him out of the class. The teacher told me that he would give me an A. because of how hard I had worked on my project, plus all of the extra time I would take after school to come work on it. I had been creating a one eyed eldritch horror monster that would be holding its eye in its hand. Pans Labyrinth combined with Monsters Inc. I had joined my High School Golf team my senior year and made some life long friends. Because I had a car, I would help out my team mates to and from the golf course after school for practices. I would even help bring some of them home after practice. 5 guys in my car with golf bags was no easy feat but we did it. At the end of the week they would pitch in 10$ each for thanking me. I told then it was nothing and I was happy to help. After one practice we all went over to hang out at my friend Cody's place. I had a great time and I was the last one to leave. As I left I didn't realize that my bully had lived in the same neighborhood that Cody did. He approached me on his bike and asked if u could take my car for a spin? I told him to leave me alone and he tried to grab my keys from my hands. I told him to back off. He just looked at me and attempted to say "What are you gonna do about it?" Before he could finish that sentence I just full on punched him in the face. He staggered and fell off his bike. I had finally enough of his shit. His nose was bleeding and I could tell that his world had finally been rocked. He finally got a taste of reality in that moment. I just stood over him and said " You don't get it do you, we're not in school anymore. I don't have to hold back my frustration and anger. You have been nothing but a thorn in my side and I've had enough." He staggered to get up and rode away on his bike as fast as he can. Cody came out of the house and asked if I was ok. I told him I was and Cody replied with " That piece of shit had it coming"
item.dc:creator: Chet1911

item.description:

I saw it on Reddit and decided if I had to endure it then so do y'all. A little interesting though.
item.dc:creator: Lethous

item.title: In nicer news...
item.description:
With all that has gone on and is continuing to go on in Ukraine, it is hard not to feel overwhelmed and extremely saddened by it. But these few posts show the side that widespread media very rarely shows; how kindhearted people can be. What a strong community.
(and if you haven't donated or read up on it yet do so!!)
item.dc:creator: ayyyolanda
